# Habeas Corpus

Habeas corpus is a legal action that lets a detained person challenge the lawfulness
of their imprisonment before a court. The name is Latin for "you shall have the
body": it requires authorities to bring the prisoner before a judge and justify the
detention. If the justification fails, the court orders the person released.

## Why it matters

Habeas corpus is regarded as a fundamental safeguard against arbitrary or indefinite
detention by the state. It does not decide guilt or innocence; it only tests whether
the detention itself is legal. Many legal systems allow the writ to be suspended only
in narrow emergencies, and such suspensions are historically controversial because
they remove a core check on government power.
